## Introduction

This one-year plan is designed to take a beginner to an intermediate level of proficiency on a musical instrument. It balances technical skill development with musical expression, ensuring consistent progress through structured practice and clear goals.

## First Half Schedule

Months 1 through 6 focus on building fundamental technique and muscle memory.
*   **Daily Practice:** 45 minutes, 6 days a week.
*   **Weeks 1-4:** Focus on posture, basic scales, and rhythm.
*   **Weeks 5-8:** Introduce simple melodies and chord progressions.
*   **Weeks 9-12:** Begin playing along with backing tracks.
*   **Weeks 13-16:** Develop dynamics and articulation.
*   **Weeks 17-24:** Consolidate skills and prepare for the first milestone performance.

## Second Half Schedule

Months 7 through 12 shift towards complexity, improvisation, and performance readiness.
*   **Daily Practice:** 60 minutes, 6 days a week.
*   **Weeks 25-28:** Learn a new piece of moderate difficulty.
*   **Weeks 29-32:** Focus on sight-reading and ear training.
*   **Weeks 33-36:** Experiment with improvisation and style variations.
*   **Weeks 37-40:** Refine performance techniques and stage presence.
*   **Weeks 41-48:** Finalize repertoire for the end-of-year recital.

## Milestone Goals

Key achievements to track progress throughout the year.
*   **Month 3:** Master all major and minor scales at a moderate tempo.
*   **Month 6:** Perform a simple song from memory without sheet music.
*   **Month 9:** Play a piece with two hands (or equivalent complexity) with correct rhythm.
*   **Month 12:** Deliver a 10-minute recital with three distinct pieces.

## Conclusion

Consistency is the key to mastering an instrument. By adhering to this schedule and meeting the milestone goals, you will build a strong foundation for advanced study. Regular self-assessment and feedback from a teacher will ensure you stay on track.
